critic_raw: The evaluation excludes the majority of incoming tickets from the performance metrics, which makes the reported results unrepresentative of the model's full behavior. A model that declines to classify most tickets and achieves excellent performance only on its selected subset is not demonstrably better than a conservative heuristic. The harmful-error rate and precision figures are computed on the 37% that passes the threshold — this is exactly the subset the model is most confident about, which will always produce optimistic numbers.
defender_raw: The evaluation is designed for a selective-prediction system, not a universal classifier. The entire product premise is that the model abstains on uncertain cases. The metrics — harmful-error rate on auto-resolved tickets plus coverage — are the correct metrics for this system. Optimism about performance on accepted tickets is not a flaw when abstention is the intended behavior. The pre-registration fixed the threshold, the error ceiling, and the coverage floor before training. The untouched test set confirmed both system requirements were met. Measuring the model as if it were required to classify every ticket would be the wrong evaluation for this product.

debate_rounds.critic: I accept the point that this is a selective-prediction system and that measuring performance on accepted tickets plus coverage is the correct methodology when the system is designed to abstain. The key condition that makes this valid is the prospective threshold setting. If the threshold was genuinely registered before training, the evaluation is sound. The concession I make is that the concern about inflated precision is not applicable here because the design explicitly creates a high-confidence subset by construction. My residual concern is whether the abstention rate is reported granularly enough to detect systematic bias.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed on all points. The abstention rate is reported at the system level per the prompt description. Category-level analysis is a supplementary fairness consideration that does not affect the primary evaluation validity. This is a clean case of a correctly designed selective-prediction evaluation.

adjudication_raw: The critic fully concedes the methodological concern after one round once the selective-prediction design and pre-registration are acknowledged. The residual abstention-bias point is a fairness audit consideration, not a primary evaluation flaw. Defense wins cleanly.
all_issues_raised: initial concern about selective-performance reporting — fully conceded after pre-registration acknowledged
abstention bias at category level as supplementary fairness question

empirical_test.condition: Verify pre-registration predates training; confirm test set meets 1% error ceiling and 30% coverage floor for the abstaining system
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Threshold was modified after development-set review, or test set fails either pre-specified operating target
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Pre-registration documentation is auditable and predates training; test confirms both targets are met on untouched data
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Safety met but coverage barely clears the 30% floor, or threshold documentation is incomplete
